title: Blink an LED from Arduino code
description: Wire an LED to a digital pin, then write the code that blinks it. Watch the breadboard react live to the sketch you just compiled.
author: Breadboard Sim Team

steps.instructions: Select Resistor and wire it from Arduino pin D13 to a hole in the breadboard's terminal strip — this limits the current the pin has to supply.
Add the LED in series. Anode toward the resistor (same column), cathode one column over.
Complete the path back to the Arduino with a wire from the LED's cathode column to the Arduino's GND pin.
Switch to the Arduino IDE tab. This sketch configures pin 13 as an output — nothing happens yet, but it's the setup every digitalWrite() needs first.
Now make it blink: turn the pin on, wait a second, turn it off, wait a second, and repeat forever inside loop().
Click Compile, then Run. Switch back to the Breadboard view — your wired LED should blink in sync with the code, driven live by the simulated AVR chip.
